explanations.5: This checks if the firstname variable is an empty string (nothing at all), using == which compares values.
explanations.8: The dot (.) here joins two pieces of text together—it's PHP's way of concatenation, like gluing strings end-to-end.
explanations.14: This calls sayHello() without passing any argument, so $firstname will be undefined and treated as empty, resulting in 'Hello World!'.
